title: With Jayson Tatum back in the fold, these Celtics know their story is just beginning
author: Khari A. Thompson
bullets: Jayson Tatum returned to the Celtics after a 10-month absence, contributing 15 points, 12 rebounds, and 7 assists.
Celtics coach Joe Mazzulla praised Tatum's effort and mindset, noting his impact despite shooting struggles.
Celtics played against Mavericks with Tatum's return, securing a victory and maintaining second place in Eastern Conference.

title: Jayson Tatum’s return was essentially perfect: 7 takeaways as Celtics blow out Mavericks
author: Tom Westerholm
bullets: Jayson Tatum returned to the Celtics' lineup without a minutes restriction, scoring 15 points in his comeback game.
The Celtics defeated the Mavericks 120-100, with Jaylen Brown leading the team with 24 points.
Celtics center Nikola Vucevic will miss a month due to a fractured finger, requiring surgery.

title: Why Bruins made right call to stand pat at trade deadline
author: Conor Ryan
bullets: Bruins GM Don Sweeney chose not to trade future assets for short-term gains at the NHL trade deadline.
Boston made minor AHL moves, sending Brett Harrison and others for Lukas Riechel, Alexis Gendron, and Massimo Rizzo.
The team retained four first-round draft picks and is focusing on long-term rebuilding plans with promising prospects.

title: Patriots reportedly miss out on Maxx Crosby trade
author: Kaley Brown
bullets: Maxx Crosby will be traded to the Baltimore Ravens for two first-round draft picks, according to ESPN.
Despite interest, the New England Patriots couldn't match Baltimore's compensation for Maxx Crosby.
The Patriots have over $60 million in cap space to pursue other trades and free agents.

title: Report: Celtics center Nikola Vucevic to miss one month with broken finger
author: Khari A. Thompson
bullets: Celtics center Nikola Vucevic fractured his right ring finger and will be out for approximately one month.
Vucevic suffered the injury during a game against the Dallas Mavericks, playing only two minutes before exiting.
Vucevic's absence affects the Celtics' front-court depth, with Jayson Tatum returning and rookies filling in gaps.

title: Watch: Jayson Tatum receives thunderous ovation in first game back with Celtics
author: Conor Ryan
bullets: Jayson Tatum returned to the Celtics' lineup against the Mavericks after a 297-day recovery from a ruptured Achilles tendon.
Tatum played six minutes in his first quarter back, notching two assists and two rebounds initially.
He scored his first points of the season in the second quarter, contributing to the Celtics' 41-21 record.

title: Report: Patriots to trade C Garrett Bradbury to Bears
author: Kaley Brown
bullets: Patriots to trade center Garrett Bradbury to the Bears for a fifth-round pick, as reported by ESPN's Adam Schefter.
The trade is pending until the new NFL league year starts; follows Bears' need due to Drew Dalman's retirement.
The move frees $5.7 million in cap space for Patriots, facilitating over $60 million for free agency expenditures.

title: 5 must-watch movies & TV shows streaming right now
author: Kevin Slane
bullets: 'Hamnet' is now streaming on Peacock, depicting a speculative story of Shakespeare's family life.
'It Was Just an Accident' by Jafar Panahi, exploring Iranian political tensions, is available on Hulu.
Netflix is streaming 'Vladimir', a drama about academic challenges and personal scandals in a modern university setting.

title: What Joe Mazzulla said about Jayson Tatum’s return to Celtics starting lineup
author: Khari A. Thompson
bullets: Jayson Tatum returns to the Celtics lineup after nearly ten months, starting in a game against the Mavericks.
Celtics coach Joe Mazzulla praised Tatum's work ethic and communication during his rehabilitation journey.
Mazzulla expressed gratitude for Tatum's vulnerability and emphasized the importance of team cohesion moving forward.

title: Bruins trade for former first-round pick Lukas Reichel
author: Conor Ryan
bullets: Boston Bruins trade a 2026 sixth-round pick to Vancouver for forward Lukas Reichel.
Reichel, 23, was drafted 17th overall in 2020 by Chicago Blackhawks, scoring 22 goals in 188 NHL games.
Reichel will start with Providence, showing potential as a speedy forward after playing in the 2026 Olympics.
